perturbation.original_text: ideologyconsulting.co.uk In consideration of the said discussions both parties agree: 1) that this agreement does not oblige or imply any obligation on either party to enter into any transaction or further agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: ideologyconsulting.co.uk In consideration of the said discussions both parties agree: 1) that this agreement oblige both parties to enter into any transaction or further agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction. The original states that the agreement does not obligate either party to enter into any transaction. The modified text states that the agreement obliges both parties to enter into a transaction. This is an in-text contradiction, as the parties obligations are not consistent.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1) that this agreement oblige both parties to enter into any transaction or further agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the agreement does not oblige either party to enter into any transaction. The changed text states that the agreement obliges both parties to enter into a transaction. This creates a direct and clear in-text contradiction with the contradicted text, because the original wording and changed wording offer opposing statements of obligation. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.
